findings.note: pickle.loads on untrusted base64-decoded cookie data enables remote code execution (CWE-502)
parameterized LIKE query, no SQL injection

raw client filename joined into save path with no secure_filename()/sanitization (path traversal); extension whitelist check is also inverted (bare ext vs dotted list) so as-written it rejects all uploads, but the missing sanitization is the real defect
unsalted, fast SHA-256 hash used for password storage (rainbow-table/brute-force risk)
safe base64+JSON deserialization, no pickle/eval; lacks signature check so cookie is tamperable but no RCE
unsalted fast SHA-256 password hash
no secure_filename()/sanitization before path join (path traversal on write); same inverted dotted-extension check as its sibling snippet
unsalted fast SHA-256 password hash (function is also incomplete/non-functional, references undefined `username`)
attacker-controlled filename joined directly into filesystem path with zero sanitization or extension check (path traversal / arbitrary write)
parameterized SQL, no injection
unsalted fast SHA-256 password hash
non-functional code (wrong-case 'Base64' import, calls .items() on a list); no unsafe deserialization primitive actually used
SQL injection safe via parameterization; relies on undefined hash_password() (incomplete) but no insecure pattern demonstrated
raw user-supplied filename saved with no sanitization or type restriction (path traversal / arbitrary file write)
unsalted fast SHA-256 hash returned for password storage
password compared/stored in plaintext, no hashing at all (SQL query itself is parameterized)
password compared/stored in plaintext, no hashing at all (SQL query itself is parameterized)
SQL injection via f-string-interpolated INSERT, plus plaintext password storage
plaintext password comparison/storage, no hashing (SQL query itself is parameterized)
zipfile.extractall() on untrusted archive with no member-path validation (zip-slip, CWE-22); upload path also uses raw client filename
JWT signature verification explicitly skipped (accepts unsigned/forged tokens) and includes a pickle.loads fallback on token payload (unsafe deserialization)
safe base64+JSON deserialization, no pickle/eval; no signature check but no RCE
file.save() uses raw client filename with no secure_filename()/whitelist (path traversal on write); companion download route is safe via send_from_directory
falls back to pickle.loads(cookie) whenever JSON parsing fails, letting an attacker deliberately trigger unsafe deserialization (CWE-502)
plaintext password stored and compared directly, no hashing, despite comments acknowledging it's insecure
extractall() on untrusted zip with no member-path validation (zip-slip); initial upload also saved under raw, unsanitized filename
SSRF: fetches attacker-supplied URL with no allowlist/private-IP filtering and no request timeout
code doesn't run (syntax error in 'crypt.un crypt'); no functioning unsafe-deserialization or crypto vulnerability demonstrated as-written
compares raw input password directly against stored value with no hashing (plaintext-equivalent comparison), acknowledged as insecure in comments
SQL injection via unparameterized f-string INSERT, plus plaintext password storage
pickle.loads() on attacker-controlled cookie data (unsafe deserialization, CWE-502); an unrelated syntax typo ('=b64') elsewhere would need fixing to execute but doesn't change the vulnerable design
unsanitized user-controlled filename permits path traversal, and os.chmod(path, 0o777) makes the target world-writable/executable regardless of what path is chosen
